notes: Strongest moment: turn 7, where the examiner collapses the two-tier distinction to show the mandatory reply sign-off means a human touches every ticket either way, extracting the user's concession that the auto-close time savings largely evaporate — all by quoting the user's own words and never asserting a domain opinion. The collision was surfaced verbatim twice (t4, t6) and framed strictly as the claims colliding ('which gives way?'), never the person conceding. Weakest moment: the brief declares a 'sharpened' verdict while its own fields read 'session in progress,' scope 'TBD,' and next_step 'no next step yet,' a slight tension between a committed verdict and an admittedly unfinished session; the two consecutive contradiction-style probes (t4, t6) also could have diversified type a turn sooner. Verdict is honest — the user held both design claims but softened the value claim, so a grounded 'sharpened' with the time-savings assumptions flagged risky/unvalidated is well-supported and no solutioning or fabrication occurred.
